title: subscribe_events return type is bounded + Send so callers can drive the stream across threads
description: subscribe_events returns impl futures::Stream<...> + Send. Without Send, the stream cannot be driven in a tokio::spawn or moved across a thread boundary in a multi-threaded async runtime. The TUI's client_task spawns a background task that owns this stream; without Send the spawn would fail to compile. A contributor might propose removing the bound as 'unnecessary constraint'. It is not unnecessary: it is a contract guarantee that the concrete unfold stream (which holds a reqwest byte stream and a String buffer, both Send) can cross thread boundaries.
close_reason: Decision record: current implementation is correct; no code change needed
